description: Minimal Claude-Code-like Python coding agent. Features track both product work (test framework, harness scaffold) and product-roadmap work (init/audit commands, memory, skills, multi-agent, observability).

state_machine.rule: Only ./init.sh (or a direct call to a feature's 'verification' command with passing output) may set status='done'. Agents may freely move between the other three states. 'done' is sticky — to re-open a done feature, change status back to 'in-progress' AND remove the 'evidence' field.

features.name: Critical fix: stream_iter 真正流式 (不是 batch) + chat log 增量滚动
features.description: 用户报告 TUI 无流式输出、无法滚动。Root cause: stream_iter 用 yield from asyncio.run(_collect()) 把所有事件收集到一个 list 才 yield,等于 batch 模式。修复:producer thread + queue.Queue 模式,daemon thread 跑 asyncio.run(_consume()),_consume 把每个 event 立即 put 进 queue,generator body yield from queue。cancel() 加 threading.Event 信号让 producer 提前退出。state machine (content_block_start/delta/stop + tool_use) 和 malformed JSON fallback 完整保留。AsyncAnthropic.messages.stream 的事件顺序 + asyncio.run 作为 test seam 的契约兼容 (返回 list 模式仍能驱动 consumer)。

features.name: TUI Header: subagent row click → dismiss overlay + scroll ChatLog to tool call marker
features.description: Wire the subagent overlay's rows to scroll the ChatLog to the corresponding tool call marker, per spec §4.3.2. Two-part change: (1) REFACTOR — move on_subagent_start/on_subagent_end firing from loom/agent/tools.py::run_task into loom/agent/loop.py::_run_tool_block, where block.id (tool_use_id) is naturally available. The current run_task generates a fresh UUID, but the TUI's ChatLog already keys ToolCallMarker by tool_use_id (`_tool_markers[tool_use_id]`) — using two different IDs forced a brittle mapping table. Using block.id directly eliminates the mapping and makes click → scroll a direct lookup. run_task is now a thin wrapper around spawn_subagent (no callback logic). (2) UI — add SubagentRow(Static, can_focus=True) widget in loom/tui/header.py that posts Header.SubagentRowClicked(tool_use_id) on click; update HeaderOverlay._compose_subagent to yield SubagentRow widgets (was: plain Static). (3) Wire — App.on_subagent_row_clicked handler dismisses the HeaderOverlay (per spec) and scrolls ChatLog to the ToolCallMarker with that tool_use_id (via chat_log._tool_markers[tool_use_id].scroll_visible(top=True, animate=False, immediate=True) + post Update/UpdateScroll messages for guaranteed repaint, matching the pattern in _forward_scroll_to_chatlog). The existing subagent_row spec row format (`<glyph> <id> · <state> · <elapsed>`) is preserved. Tests: new cases in tests/test_tui_header.py for SubagentRow click message posting, app handler dismissing overlay + scrolling, run_task no longer fires callback, _run_tool_block fires for task tools only, callback receives block.id. Eval: new cases in loom/eval/cases/tui_header.py for SubagentRow class defined, Header.SubagentRowClicked message defined, App.on_subagent_row_clicked handler defined, _run_tool_block fires subagent callbacks for task tools. Per spec §4.3.2: 'Clicking a subagent ID inside the Subagent overlay dismisses the overlay and scrolls the ChatLog to that subagent's existing marker. (Markers exist because subagent tool calls are already inline in the chat.)'

features.name: TUI StatusBar: drop the 'scroll with mouse wheel' hint for narrow-terminal usability
features.description: Closes the §7 narrow-terminal layout decision. Empirical measurement (App rendered at 60/70/80/90/100/120 cols) showed the StatusBar is the only region that breaks under narrow width — Header (3× width:1fr buttons), ChatLog (soft-wrap markdown, overflow-x hidden), and Composer (TextArea soft-wrap) all scale gracefully. The StatusBar is fixed-format non-wrapping text; its length grew to 119 chars once the chat log overflowed because of the appended ' | scroll with mouse wheel' hint (added in f-tui-mouse-wheel-scroll). Dropping the hint reduces max StatusBar width from 119 → 93 cols (85 at session start). Mouse-wheel scrolling is the default intuitive behavior, so the hint mostly added width. features.name: TUI Header: per-section toggle + collapse mechanisms (UX fix)
features.description: Refactor of f-tui-header-summary-rail (commit 61cda27) to fix two UX issues user reported 2026-06-20: (a) MCP/todo/subagent sections shared one expand key — clicking once expanded all three. Now each section is an independently clickable HeaderSectionButton; clicking expands only that section; clicking another section switches; clicking same collapses. Only ONE overlay visible at a time (mutual exclusion). (b) After expansion there was no collapse mechanism. Now: ESC key collapses (per spec §4.3.2), clicking the same section button again collapses, clicking anywhere outside the overlay (chat log/status bar/composer) collapses. Click on overlay CONTENT itself does NOT collapse (user is reading). Implementation: Header refactored from Static to Horizontal with 3 HeaderSectionButton children (mcp/todo/subagent); HeaderOverlay now takes (section, state) constructor args and renders only that section (was rendering all 3 sections previously); App gets new BINDING escape → action_collapse_header, new on_click handler that consumes Header/HeaderOverlay clicks and collapses on any other click, new on_header_section_toggle handler with expand/switch/collapse logic; per-section overlay IDs (header-overlay-mcp/todo/subagent) avoid DuplicateIds on switch (old overlay may still be in DOM pending async removal). features.name: TUI test reliability: wait_for_state helper + App.on_event ScreenStackError defense
features.description: Fix pre-existing test flake in wheel-scroll tests (test_status_bar.py × 4 + test_tui_manual_scroll.py × 3) where `assert chat_log.scroll_y < baseline` raced with a fixed `await pilot.pause(0.1)`. Replace the fixed pause with a poll-until-true helper. Also fix a related production robustness issue surfaced by the change: `AgentTUIApp.on_event` did not defend against `ScreenStackError` when `super().on_event(event)` called `self.screen._forward_event(event)` after the screen stack had been torn down (race during message-pump teardown). The flake manifested as `assert 115 < 115` under system load.
features.verification: ./init.sh && uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100 (3 consecutive runs)
features.evidence: uv run pytest -q → 453 passed (3 consecutive runs: 85-91s each, no flakes); uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100 → 224/224 passed (3 consecutive runs, ~5min each, no flakes); uv run python -m loom.cli audit . → 100/100, all 6 dimensions 5/5; ./init.sh → 'Verification Complete (all green)'; uv run ruff check . → clean; uv run mypy loom/ → clean. Implementation: (1) tests/conftest.py — added `async def wait_for_state(pilot, predicate, *, timeout=2.0, interval=0.02, message='') -> None` that polls until the predicate is truthy or raises AssertionError with the final predicate value; (2) tests/test_status_bar.py — replaced 4 fixed `pilot.pause(0.1)` / `pilot.pause(0.2)` calls after wheel events with `wait_for_state(predicate=lambda: chat_log.scroll_y < baseline, timeout=2.0)`; (3) tests/test_tui_manual_scroll.py — same for 3 tests (up, down, bubble-from-markdown); (4) loom/tui/app.py — added defensive `try/except ScreenStackError: return` around `super().on_event(event)` and `from textual.app import ScreenStackError`. Note: the project pin textual>=0.85.0 resolved to 8.2.7 (a custom/forked build) which has no `Pilot.wait_for` API; `wait_for_state` is a self-contained substitute that mirrors the same predicate-based wait semantic. NOT a behavior change in any user-facing code path — the wheel test assertions are still 'chat_log scrolled by the wheel event' but now wait for the actual outcome instead of a guessed delay. The App.on_event defense is a one-line production change that swallows the screen-stale race during teardown; the event is dropped (no action) because there is no screen to act on it.

features.name: TUI: fire-and-forget init.sh on SessionEnd + 2nd Ctrl-D cancel
features.description: TUI action_quit uses P1 schedule_init_sh_on_session_end helper (no block). Adds 'init.sh running...' status + _on_init_sh_complete banner via chat_log.append_system_note (file-not-found/timeout/pass/fail mappings). Helper gains stop_event param. Two new eval cases verify end-to-end subprocess timing + banner spy via pilot. TUI exit: 48s → < 0.5s.
features.verification: ./init.sh && uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100
features.evidence: All gates green: uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100 → 'Eval results: 230/230 passed' (was 226/226, +4 new P2 cases: stop-event-terminates-running-init-sh, stop-event-none-backward-compat, tui-quit-does-not-block-on-init-sh, tui-init-sh-completion-banner-runs); uv run pytest tests/ -q → 453 passed, 36 warnings in 144.87s; ./init.sh → 'Verification Complete (all green)'; uv run python -m loom.cli audit . → 'Overall: 100/100'; uv run ruff check . → All checks passed!; uv run mypy loom/ → 'Success: no issues found in 80 source files'. Manual smoke (THE critical test — TUI exit time with slow init.sh): init.sh = 'sleep 60', time (loom tui & sleep 0.5; kill -TERM $!; wait $!) → 0.513s total; time (loom tui & sleep 1.0; kill -TERM $!; wait $!) → 1.013s total. Down from baseline 48s; plan target was < 3s. Refactor: (1) loom/agent/loop.py — +stop_event: threading.Event | None = None keyword-only param in schedule_init_sh_on_session_end; +_terminate_proc (SIGTERM → 2s → SIGKILL); _runner uses subprocess.Popen + 0.5s polling loop when stop_event is set, checks is_set() + elapsed timeout, fires on_complete(error_msg='stopped'|'timed out'); default None → backward-compatible subprocess.run path (used by REPL). (2) loom/tui/app.py — +import threading; +'from __future__ import annotations'; +self._init_sh_thread: threading.Thread | None = None; action_quit REWRITTEN to use schedule_init_sh_on_session_end(WORKDIR, _active_config, on_complete=on_done, timeout=120.0), on_done uses self.call_from_thread for safe main-loop hop, self.exit() called immediately (no subprocess.run, no thread.join); +_on_init_sh_complete method with 5-case mapping (file-not-found/stopped silent, timed out → TIMEOUT, rc=0 → pass, else → FAIL with stderr_tail[-200:]). (3) loom/eval/cases/stop_event_helper.py NEW (2 cases — helper-level). (4) loom/eval/cases/tui_async_init_sh.py NEW (2 cases — TUI-level: mocked sleep(60) daemon thread + asyncio.run(action_quit()) wall-time check; pilot mode + chat_log.append_system_note spy + direct _on_init_sh_complete call). (5) loom/eval/cases/__init__.py — +2 lines (register both new modules alphabetically). (6) feature_list.json — +1 entry (this one). (7) progress.md — +P2 session section. First-pass mistake caught by mypy: action_quit was set to sync `def`; reverted to `async def` (no `await` in body) to satisfy Textual's base-class override contract. Foundation: P3 (cli lazy imports) and P4 (REPL async exit) can now proceed.
